output:
CVE-2024-26154 refers to a vulnerability in ETIC Telecom Remote Access Server (RAS) versions prior to 4.5.0, specifically involving a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) issue in the appliance site name functionality. This vulnerability, classified under CWE-79, arises from improper neutralization of user-controllable input before it is utilized on web pages, potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary scripts within the context of the user's browser session.

The severity level of this vulnerability is classified as MEDIUM. It impacts any version of the remote access server firmware below 4.5.0, posing a threat to administrators who may unwittingly trigger script execution when interacting with the web interface.

To mitigate CVE-2024-26154, administrators should ensure systems are updated to version 4.5.0 or later to close this security gap. Employing additional security measures such as input validation (M1050), output encoding (M1051), and web application firewalls (M1048) can further help protect against similar threats. The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique, T1203, highlights the use of exploitation for user execution.
